content: DSM-5-TR criteria: Recurrent unexpected panic attacks (abrupt surge of intense fear/discomfort peaking within minutes with ≥4 symptoms: palpitations, sweating, trembling, dyspnea, choking, chest pain, nausea, dizziness, chills/heat, paresthesias, derealization, fear of losing control/going crazy, fear of dying). ≥1 attack followed by ≥1 month of: persistent concern about additional attacks/consequences, or significant maladaptive behavioral change related to attacks. Disturbance not attributable to substance effects, medical condition, or better explained by another mental disorder (social anxiety, specific phobia, OCD, PTSD). Agoraphobia diagnosed separately if present.
